skill.name: url-citation-search
skill.description: Find papers and preprints that cite a given URL, especially blogs, docs pages, project pages, or other web content that standard citation indexes often miss. Use when the user provides a URL and wants confirmed citing works, evidence from PDF or HTML references, DOI or arXiv links, BibTeX, or a deduplicated citation report.
skill.summary: Find academic citations for web content URLs that standard indexes miss.
skill.author: zjsxply

security_audit.summary: Security analysis confirmed no security risks. The static scanner flagged 25 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' and 2 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' - both were false positives. The backtick detections are markdown code block delimiters (```), not code execution. The cryptographic flag was an incorrect pattern match on the word 'MIT' in the license field. The skill is a research documentation guide that provides instructions for searching academic citations via web searches. No executable code, network exfiltration, or malicious patterns were found.

content.user_title: Find Academic Citations for Any URL
content.value_statement: Standard citation indexes miss web pages like blogs and docs. This skill finds academic papers that cite any URL by searching across multiple sources and verifying each citation.
actual_capabilities: Search across academic sources including arXiv, OpenReview, ACL, NeurIPS, IEEE, and Springer
Verify citations by inspecting PDF text, HTML reference lists, and bibliography metadata
Handle URL variants including http/https, www/non-www, moved domains, and mirrored URLs
Return deduplicated citation reports with title, year, type, and landing links
Generate BibTeX entries for citing papers on request
Distinguish between direct citations, official variants, and mirror citations
limitations: Cannot access paywalled papers without existing access
PDF text extraction may miss some citations due to lossy extraction
Cannot verify citations that only exist in printed versions of papers
Performance depends on web search quality and paper availability

use_cases.title: Track Research Impact
use_cases.description: Researchers can discover which academic papers cite their blog posts, documentation, or open-source project pages to understand broader influence beyond traditional citation indexes.

use_cases.title: Literature Review
use_cases.description: Graduate students and researchers can find relevant papers that build upon specific tools, frameworks, or methodologies documented online, complementing traditional keyword searches.

use_cases.title: Verify Academic Influence
use_cases.description: Authors can confirm when their online content or preprints have been cited in formal publications, useful for tenure dossiers and grant applications.

prompt_templates.title: Find Papers Citing a Blog Post
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to find academic papers that cite this URL: [URL]. Please search for confirmed citations and provide a citation report with DOI or arXiv links.

prompt_templates.title: Get BibTeX for Citing Papers
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all papers that cite https://example.com/blog/post and return the BibTeX entries for each citing paper.

prompt_templates.title: Track Documentation Impact
prompt_templates.prompt: My project documentation at [URL] has been cited by academic papers. Find the confirmed citations, distinguish direct from mirror citations, and list them with evidence notes.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Platform Citation Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for papers that cite this URL: [URL]. Check arXiv, IEEE, Springer, and ACM. Verify each citation by inspecting the PDF or HTML reference list. Return only confirmed citations with their evidence.

output_examples.output:
Confirmed Citations:

1. Neural Methods for Text Classification (2023) - arXiv:2301.12345
   Type: Preprint
   Evidence: URL found in PDF reference section
   Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.12345

2. Deep Learning Approaches in NLP (2024) - DOI:10.1234/example
   Type: Published paper
   Evidence: URL in HTML bibliography metadata
   Link: https://doi.org/10.1234/example

best_practices: Always verify citations against the actual PDF or HTML reference list rather than relying on search snippets alone
Check canonical URL redirects and mirrored URLs to capture all possible citations of the same content
Distinguish between direct citations and mirror citations in the final report to avoid duplicate entries
anti_patterns: Do not count papers that merely discuss similar topics as citations - require visible reference to the URL or page title
Do not trust publisher landing page snippets alone for verification - inspect the actual bibliography
Do not assume published versions preserve web citations seen in preprints - verify each version separately

faq.question: What types of URLs work best with this skill?
faq.answer: Blog posts, documentation pages, project pages, GitHub README files, and other web content that researchers commonly cite but standard indexes miss.

faq.question: How does this skill verify citations?
faq.answer: It checks PDF text extraction, HTML reference metadata, bibliography pages, and arXiv external link annotations to confirm the target URL appears in the citing work.

faq.question: Can I find citations for paywalled papers?
faq.answer: The skill can search for and identify citations but may not access PDF content for verification if the paper is behind a paywall.

faq.question: What academic sources are searched?
faq.answer: The skill searches arXiv, OpenReview, ACL Anthology, NeurIPS, MLR Press, ACM DL, IEEE Xplore, Nature, Springer, and CEUR-WS.

faq.question: How does it handle moved or mirrored content?
faq.answer: The skill follows canonical URL redirects and tracks mirror URLs to ensure citations pointing to old or mirrored versions are captured and labeled accordingly.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Brief lists (title, year, type, link), full citation reports (with DOI/arXiv IDs and evidence notes), and BibTeX entries for citing papers.
